history.content:
<thought>
There is a wall (#) directly in front of the agent (column C is all # and the agent at row 07 is facing toward that barrier). To avoid it, I will turn right to change direction toward the open cells on the right side.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can see my row 07 column D has the agent and there is an open cell directly in front at row 07 column D’s forward direction is toward column C (which is L at row 07 col C). Since the cell ahead is lava (L), I must not move forward. The cell to the left (toward column E) is a wall (#), so I will turn left to face away from the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
From my current position (row 07, column D) I see a vertical wall at column C (#) so I should not move left into it. Lava is present at column D ahead (L above), so moving forward would be deadly. The safe option is to turn right to head toward column E where the cells are floor (.) and eventually toward the goal region.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The wall column is at C (#) and is to my left/right depending on orientation, but from this view my forward cell is lava: row 07 is at my level and the cell ahead (row 06, col D) is L. Since MOVE_FORWARD would step onto lava, I should avoid it. I’ll turn right so I can look for a floor cell rather than lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
My view has shifted: there’s a solid wall row at line 06 across columns A-E (#). I am currently on row 07, column D facing upward toward that wall, and the cell directly in front should be a wall, so MOVE_FORWARD would be impossible. To look for open floor cells, I’ll turn left to change direction away from the wall row.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I can see lava (L) in column C ahead of me on rows 02-06, and there is a wall at column E (#). The cell directly in front of my position (row 08, column D) isn’t shown, but given the wall arrangement, moving forward would likely be unsafe. To avoid lava/walls and look for the goal, I’ll turn right to scan adjacent cells.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The local map view updated and now shows a solid wall row (row 06 columns A-E are #), and I am on row 07 column D. The cell in front (row 08, column D) is not shown in this view, but since the only nearby visible blocked area is directly above, the safest next step is to turn right to move along the open floor area at row 07 rather than toward the wall row.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column D (^) with a lava cell (L) visible to my right at row 07 column E, and floor cells at row 07 columns F and G. Since moving forward would likely hit lava or a wall, I’ll turn left to face the safe floor area instead of the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I see the wall boundary shifted: I’m at row 07 column D (^) and the cell to my right (row 07 column E) is a wall (#). The safest option is to turn left so I can face open floor (row 07 columns C or D direction changes away from the wall) rather than move forward into the wall.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front is row 06 column D which is floor (.) and not lava. Moving forward should be safe and progress toward the goal area.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
Ahead (row 06 column D) is floor '.', so I can move forward safely and continue deeper into the open area.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column D facing up. The cell in front (row 06 column D) is floor '.', not lava, so moving forward is safe.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
Facing up, the cell directly ahead is row 06 column D, which is floor '.' (not lava and not a wall). I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column D facing up. The cell ahead (row 06 column D) is 'L' lava, so I cannot move forward. I should turn right to find a safer path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing right. The cell to my right (row 07 column E) is '.' floor, which is safe. I will move forward into it.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I appear to have moved into a position where the local map still shows a wall directly ahead (the entire row 06 is walls). Since I cannot move forward into a wall, I should turn to search for a safe direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is not visible yet. I’m currently at row 07 column D facing up (based on `^` in the bottom-center). The cell directly in front (row 06 column D) is `L` lava, so I must not move forward. I should turn right to face a safer direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Now the local view shows my position at row 07 column D with `^` again, and the cell directly in front (row 06 column D) is `#` wall, so I cannot move forward. I should turn right to try to find an adjacent safe floor toward the open area.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can see a vertical corridor with walls on the left (#) and a column of lava (L) to my right in front-facing directions. From the view, the cell in front of me (row 6 same column) is `.` (safe), while moving forward is not directly into `L`/`#`. So I should move forward one step.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal `G` is not visible yet. I am at row 7 column D facing up (since the `L` is at row 7 column E to my right). The cell in front of me (row 6 column D) is `.` so it is safe to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
From my current position (row 7 column D) facing up, the front cell is row 6 column D which is `.` (safe). The cell at row 6 column E is `L` to the right, so I should avoid turning right into lava. I will continue forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
My forward cell (row 5 column D) is currently outside the visible map as row 5 is not shown beyond walls; however, the visible area indicates that the row 5 cells are all walls (`#`) starting from column C to G. That means I cannot proceed forward without hitting a wall. I will turn right to look for an opening not blocked by lava/walls.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can see a vertical wall block at column B (`#`). My current position is at row 7 column D facing up. Forward (row 6 column D) is `L`, so I cannot move forward. The cell to my right (facing up => right is east, row 7 column E) is `.` (safe). I will turn right to face it.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing up and the forward cell (row 6, column D) is `.` (safe). The left/right are less clear, but since the path ahead isn’t lava or a wall, I should move forward toward the goal region.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD

<thought>
Forward (row 6, column D relative to my view) is `.` and not a wall or lava, so moving ahead is safe and keeps me progressing through the corridor.
